attacking_style: Launches repeated accelerations in final 4km of summit finishes rather than single long-range moves. Prefers explosive 500m kicks inside final kilometer on steep gradients, targeting stage wins and bonus seconds simultaneously.
team_tactics: Operates with minimal visible team support in mountain finishes; relies on rivals' tempo or positioning himself in early breaks rather than domestique-led trains. Increasingly stage-hunts from breakaways when not in GC contention.
terrain_preference: Steep gradient summit finishes where repeated accelerations wear down opponents. Struggles on uncategorized rises before finishes and completely exposed in time trials of any length (consistently loses 1+ minute to top GC riders).
vulnerability: Chronic time trial weakness costs 30+ seconds per 20km effort. Poor positioning in crosswinds (lost 2+ minutes in routine flat stage). Recurring injury issues force mid-race abandonments when in winning positions. Cannot sustain late chases in final kilometers.
key_patterns: Repeated accelerations tactic: multiple observations of serial attacks vs single move (Lombardia 2018, Tourmalet 2019)
Time trial liability: consistently loses 30-50 seconds minimum in any ITT discipline across all years
Breakaway stage-hunting: when not GC threat, targets KOM points then abandons or soft-pedals back
Bonus second obsession: attacks after finish line and in intermediate sprints for marginal GC gains
Injury-prone: multiple abandonments while in genuine contention (Tour 2019 knee, Tour 2020 back)
Poor crosswind positioning: catastrophic time loss in routine flat stages exposes team/tactical weakness
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 observations show strategic break riding for KOM points rather than contesting, couldn't finish sprints despite animating stages, treating races as training rather than competing for results
built_from: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 8
